  3. Canadian passports and other travel documents

Find a passport or travel document form

Only use passport forms from this website (Canada.ca). Forms on other sites may not be official or up to date.

Always download a new form each time you submit an application. We may reject your application if you don’t use the latest official form.

Get the forms you need for your application

Answer the questions below. We’ll then give you all the forms you need for your application.

Get large-print forms or braille instructions
  • In Canada or the US Contact us online (opens in a new tab) or call 1-800-567-6868 (TTY: 1-866-255-7655).
    • Tell us whether you need a large print form or braille instructions, and your mailing address. We’ll mail you the forms.
  • Outside Canada and the US: contact the closest Canadian government office abroad.

Select an application type

  • Passport

    What kind of passport?

    • Adult passport renewal (16 and older)

      Where are you applying from?

      If you live in Bermuda, American Samoa, the Midway Islands, Puerto Rico or the US Virgin Islands, select United States. The same steps apply to these countries.

      • Canada
      • United States
      • Outside Canada and the United States
    • New adult passport (16 and older)

      Do you already have a passport?

      • Yes

        You may be able to renew your passport. Renewing a passport is simpler than applying for a new one.

        What do you want to do?

      If your child needs a passport, you must apply for a new one. Even if they already have one, you can’t renew it.

How to open our forms

You need to

  • use your computer
    • The forms may not open on mobile devices (iPads, tablets, mobile phones).
  • save the file on your computer in a place you can remember
  • use Adobe Reader 10 or higher to open the file on your computer
    • If you don’t have it, download Adobe Reader 10 for free.
    • Adobe has help for solving common issues with Reader.

For more help, see our step-by-step instructions on downloading and opening PDFs.

If you still have trouble opening the forms, contact us online or call us at 1-800-567-6868. If you’re outside Canada or the United States, contact the closest Canadian government office abroad.
